name: Make webhooks reliable when downstream is flaky
description: Stripe sends payment webhooks to our API. Our downstream services (email, analytics, CRM) sometimes fail or time out. We've been losing payment state updates when downstream errors cascade.
task: Design a reliable webhook processing system. Stripe webhooks come in at ~200/min. For each webhook we need to: (1) update our DB, (2) send a confirmation email via SendGrid, (3) track in analytics (Segment), (4) sync to CRM (HubSpot). Any of 2-4 can fail or time out. Currently we're losing state. Requirements: (1) never lose a webhook, (2) respond to Stripe within 3s, (3) retry failed downstream calls with backoff, (4) visibility into failures, (5) exactly-once DB update. Give me the architecture + key code patterns.
expected_outcomes: Separates webhook receipt from downstream fan-out (queue pattern)
Idempotency key handling to prevent double processing
Dead letter queue for downstream failures
